violations.text: Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (Ice machine) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Ice was emptied from machine, ice was voluntarily disposed of. Establishment to send photo of cleaned and sanitized ice machine to DOH for approval to resume use. New Violation. Correct By: 04/08/2025

Food items prepared in advance were observed not marked with the date of preparation. Ready-to-eat time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods prepared in the establishment and held for more than 24 hours must be marked with the date of preparation. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/08/2025

Ground beef was observed thawing on the counter. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods must be thawed in one of the following manners: under refrigeration (41 degrees Fahrenheit or less); completely submerged under running water; as part of the cooking process; or in the microwave if cooked immediately after thawing. Beef was 36 DF, was moved to Walk in cooler. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/08/2025

The cutting boards in the kitchen are very scratched and scored. Cutting blocks and boards that are subject to scratching and scoring shall be resurfaced if they can no longer be effectively cleaned and sanitized, or discarded if they are not capable of being resurfaced. New Violation. Correct By: 04/15/2025
The non food contact surfaces of the buffalo chopper were observed in poor repair. Equipment must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 05/08/2025
The facility is lacking test strips to measure the concentration of the chlorine sanitizing solution for the 3 bay. New Violation. Correct By: 04/15/2025

The sink used for food preparation is lacking an air gap. A direct connection must not exist between the sewage system and a drain originating from equipment in which food is placed. New Violation. Correct By: 04/15/2025

Mashed potatoes and marinara sauce were observed held at 115 degrees Fahrenheit at the hot hold unit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be maintained at 135 degrees Fahrenheit or above except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Food item was reheated during inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 01/30/2024
No person certified in food safety was present as needed at the time of inspection. New Violation. Correct By: 01/09/2024
The establishment does not have a written procedure for cleaning vomit and diarrhea. New Violation. Correct By: 01/09/2024

An open employees' beverage container was observed stored in a food preparation area. Employees shall not drink from open beverage containers in order to prevent contamination of their hands, the container, and exposed food. -Staff removed the open beverage container during this inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 01/09/2024

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (can opener) observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. -Staff washed, rinsed, and sanitized the can opener during this inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 01/09/2024

Chlorine sanitizer solution for wiping cloths observed in excess of 200 parts per million (ppm). A chlorine sanitizer solution applied to food contact surfaces must be between 50-100 ppm. Staff made a new solution during this inspection. Chlorine sanitizer solution corrected and observed at 50-100ppm. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 01/09/2024

The door to the outside, located in the back, is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 01/30/2024

The non-food contact surfaces of the ice machine and floors in the walk-in cooler have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. The non-food contact surfaces shall be cleaned as often as necessary to prevent buildup of residue. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 01/09/2024

The sink used for food preparation is lacking an air gap. A direct connection must not exist between the sewage system and a drain originating from equipment in which food is placed. New Violation. Correct By: 01/23/2024

The wall in the back kitchen area, ceiling tiles, and some floor tiles in the kitchen area observed in poor repair. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. -Floors/walls/ceilings shall be smooth, durable, and easily cleanable. New Violation. Correct By: 02/29/2024

Inadequate lighting observed in the walk-in cooler. Lighting 6-303.11 Intensity. The light intensity shall be: (A) At least 108 lux (10 foot candles) at a distance of 75 cm (30 inches) above the floor, in walk-in refrigeration units and dry FOOD storage areas and in other areas and rooms during periods of cleaning. Lighting 6-303.11 Intensity. Lighting levels are specified so that sufficient light is available to enable employees to perform certain functions such as reading labels; discerning the color of substances; identifying toxic materials; recognizing the condition of food, utensils, and supplies; and safely conducting general food establishment operations and clean-up. Properly distributed light makes the need for cleaning apparent by making accumulations of soil conspicuous. New Violation. Correct By: 01/30/2024
